headline: Visa (V) Stock After Recent Pullback Is The Market Price Missing The Story
summary: If you are wondering whether Visa stock is priced fairly or not, the key is understanding how its current market value compares with several valuation checks. Visa shares last closed at US$330.38, with returns of 2.3% over the past week, a slight decline of 0.7% over the past month, a larger decline of 4.6% year to date, and declines of 2.2% over 1 year, while showing gains of 48.7% over 3 years and 46.1% over 5 years. Recent price moves in Visa stock have come alongside ongoing interest in...

headline: 3 Mega-Cap Stocks with Exciting Potential
summary: Megacap stocks dominate their sectors and their actions influence economies worldwide. The flip side though is that their sheer size means they have less room for explosive growth as scale works against them.

headline: Is Visa (V) a Buy as Wall Street Analysts Look Optimistic?
summary: Based on the average brokerage recommendation (ABR), Visa (V) should be added to one's portfolio. Wall Street analysts' overly optimistic recommendations cast doubt on the effectiveness of this highly sought-after metric. So, is the stock worth buying?

headline: Coverd Launches the First Credit Card Built Around Instant, Everyday Rewards
summary: Coverd, the credit card company reimagining personal finance for the next generation, today announced the launch of the Coverd Card, the first credit card offering up to 100% cash back through a transparent earn-back mechanism built into every swipe. Issued in partnership with Rain, the global stablecoin payments platform for enterprises, the Coverd Card is accepted at over 150 million merchants worldwide, wherever Visa is accepted.
